chunks.text: Industry-Oriented Graduate Programs - EECS at Berkeley M.Eng students Compare Industry-Oriented Graduate Degree Programs Berkeley EECS has two professional/industry-oriented Master’s degree programs for students with a variety of objectives. Master of Engineering (M.Eng.) Semester admitted: Fall (August) Application deadline: Early January Commitment: Full-time 1 year Location: On-Campus Focus: Technical courses + engineering leadership curriculum The MEng program is a one year professional Master’s degree. The program focuses on developing essential leadership skills while deepening the technical foundation. The MEng program is specifically streamlined for students looking to enter into industry after graduation. More about M.Eng. program Five Year Master of Science (M.S.) Berkeley undergraduates only Semester admitted: Fall (August) Application deadline: Mid-December Commitment: Full-time 1 year Location: On-Campus Focus: Continued research project This program is for Berkeley EECS/CDSS CS undergraduates only and requires a continued research project with an EECS faculty member. It is not intended for students who have definitely decided to pursue a Ph.D. More about the 5th Year M.S. program View Open Faculty Positions
